entries.d: a person who plays the keyboard
entries.s: The _____ in our band sits at an electric piano and plays with both hands during every song.

entries.d: a musical instrument with keys
entries.s: She plays the _____ with her fingers on all 88 black and white keys.

entries.d: a person who sings songs
entries.s: The _____ holds the microphone and uses her voice to sing the words of each song.

entries.d: a person who plays the drums
entries.s: The _____ sits behind the drum kit and hits the drums very fast with two wooden sticks.

entries.d: a musical instrument you hit with sticks
entries.s: He plays the _____ with two sticks and hits them very hard to make loud beats.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with DRUMS?

entries.d: a person who plays the saxophone
entries.s: The _____ holds a shiny brass instrument in her mouth and blows into it to play jazz music.

entries.d: a metal instrument you blow into
entries.s: She wants to learn the _____ this year — a big golden wind instrument you blow into.

entries.d: a person who plays the guitar
entries.s: The _____ in the band plays the guitar with a small plastic pick in his right hand.

entries.d: a string instrument you play with your hands
entries.s: He got a new electric _____ with six strings for his birthday and now he practices every day.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with GUITAR?

entries.d: a boy who is your special friend
entries.s: Anna has a new _____ called Jack. He is a boy and he is her special friend.

entries.d: belonging to a thing or animal
entries.s: The dog is wagging _____ tail because it is happy to see me.

entries.d: to make music or have fun
entries.s: Can you _____ the guitar and show me how to hold it?

entries.d: a live music show
entries.s: We are going to a rock _____ on Saturday night to hear my favourite band play live on stage.

entries.d: to move your body to music
entries.s: I love to _____ to the music when I hear my favourite song — I move my feet and arms.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with DANCE?

entries.d: telling someone not to be afraid
entries.s: _____ Everything will be fine in the morning. I promise you there is no problem.

entries.d: work that you do
entries.s: My mum has a good _____ at the hospital. She is a doctor and earns money to pay for our food and house.

entries.d: the best it can be, no mistakes
entries.s: Your English test is _____! You got ten correct answers out of ten, with zero mistakes!

entries.d: to hold something and move with it
entries.s: Can you help me _____ these very heavy bags of shopping from the car into the house?

entries.d: to put your feet on something
entries.s: Please don't _____ the flowers in the garden.

entries.d: the soft part inside your mouth
entries.s: The doctor said: 'Open your mouth and stick out your _____ so I can see your throat.'

entries.d: to put your hand on something
entries.s: Please don't _____ the old paintings in the museum with your fingers — they are very delicate.

entries.d: to move on your feet over something
entries.s: Be very careful when you _____ the thin ice on the frozen pond — it might crack.

entries.d: to move something from side to side quickly
entries.s: Can you _____ your ears up and down? I can't! I can only move my eyebrows.

entries.d: a metal container for food or drinks
entries.s: He drinks a _____ of lemonade every day.

entries.d: to take water or juice into your mouth
entries.s: You must _____ a lot of water when it is very hot outside and you are running around.

entries.d: the number 100
entries.s: There are one _____ children in our whole school — 50 boys and 50 girls.

entries.d: all at one time, not stopping
entries.s: He was very hungry so he ate the whole pizza _____ without stopping between slices.

entries.d: asking if something is really true
entries.s: I can eat ten huge apples in one minute! — _____ That's hard to believe!

entries.d: introducing yourself
entries.s: Look at the old photo from when I was 5 years old. _____ The little boy in the blue shirt.

entries.d: the system of money and business
entries.s: We learn about the _____ in our new social studies class — how money, shops, and jobs work.

entries.d: a place where sick people go
entries.s: My grandma is very sick with a broken leg. She is in the _____ where the doctors and nurses take care of her.

entries.d: to make a happy sound, ha ha ha
entries.s: The clown at the circus is very funny and we all _____ out loud when he falls over.

entries.d: money your parents give you each week
entries.s: I get five euros _____ from my parents every Saturday for doing my chores.

entries.d: money used in Britain
entries.s: This British book costs five _____ — the official money of the UK. Not euros.

entries.d: the money you make from selling something
entries.s: She sells homemade lemonade at her stall. After paying for the lemons and sugar, she makes a _____ of ten euros.

entries.d: a place at school where you eat lunch
entries.s: We eat our hot lunch together in the _____ at 12 o'clock with all the other pupils.

entries.d: a piece of furniture with legs and a flat top
entries.s: Please put the dinner plates and glasses on the big wooden _____ with four legs where we eat.

entries.d: a person who helps you learn at school
entries.s: Our class _____ Mrs. Smith gives us new homework every day and marks our tests.

entries.d: your mum's or dad's brother
entries.s: My _____ lives in London. He is my dad's brother and visits us every Christmas.

entries.d: to clean with water
entries.s: Please _____ your dirty hands with soap and water before you sit down to eat dinner.

entries.d: not anything
entries.s: There is _____ in the cardboard box. It is completely empty — not even a piece of paper.

entries.d: asking someone to say it again
entries.s: _____ I did not hear what you said. Can you repeat it a little louder, please?
